channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: 2 charged in deadly drone attack on US soldiers in Jordan
item.description: The U.S. on Monday charged two Iranian officials in a scheme that contributed to a deadly January drone attack that killed three American service members in Jordan. The Department of Justice (DOJ) charged Mahdi Mohammad Sadeghi, 42, who is a U.S.-Iranian national from Natick, Mass.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Netanyahu spokesperson denies Cairo trip amid Gaza ceasefire push
item.description: A spokesperson for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Tuesday rejected reports the Israeli leader was traveling to Cairo for negotiations over a ceasefire and hostage release deal with Hamas in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Russian general accused of chemical weapon use killed by Moscow bomb
item.description: A top Russian general in charge of Russia's nuclear defense forces was killed on Tuesday by a bomb hidden inside his scooter. Russia's Investigative Committee confirmed the death of Lt. Gen. Igor Kirillov and said it was being investigated under terrorism and other charges.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: DHS overhauls H-1B visa program
item.description: The Biden administration announced a rule Tuesday clarifying who can apply for an H-1B work visa, a key program to attract international talent that’s been criticized as too complicated and susceptible to abuse.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, The Hill

item.title: Filipina who won a last-minute reprieve from the Indonesian firing squad in 2015 is to return home
item.description: Indonesian authorities are to return a Filipina woman to the Philippines, who was on death row in Indonesia and was nearly executed by firing squad in 2015, under an arrangement between the two countries.
item.dc:creator: Niniek Karmini And Andi Jatmiko, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 11 Indian citizens dead in Georgian ski resort accident, embassy says
item.description: Eleven Indian citizens were among 12 victims of a deadly accident at an Indian restaurant in the Georgian ski resort of Gudauri, the Indian Embassy said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Father and stepmother sentenced to life in prison for the murder of a 10-year-old girl in England
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The father and stepmother of a severely abused 10-year-old girl found dead in her home in England were sentenced Tuesday to life in prison for murder.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US Embassy seriously damaged in Vanuatu earthquake
item.description: A forceful 7.4 magnitude earthquake shook the capital of Vanuatu damaging the newly established U.S. Embassy on Tuesday. Authorities reported the earthquake and subsequent aftershocks at 12:53 p.m. local time, according to an embassy alert.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Canada's finance minister resigns as unpopular Trudeau faces biggest test of his political career
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau faced the biggest test of his political career after Finance Minister Chrystia Freeland, long one of his most powerful and loyal ministers, resigned from the Cabinet on Monday.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Father and stepmother sentenced to life in prison for the murder of a 10-year-old girl in England
item.description: The father and stepmother of a severely abused 10-year-old girl found dead in her home in England have been sentenced to life in prison for murder.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Authorities impose overnight curfew in cyclone-ravaged Mayotte as France rushes in aid
item.description: An overnight curfew goes into force Tuesday evening as Mayotte struggles to recover from Cyclone Chido, the strongest storm to hit the French Indian Ocean archipelago in 90 years.
item.dc:creator: Adrienne Surprenant And Tom Nouvian, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Battle erupts over South Korean court that will determine the fate of impeached Yoon
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s acting leader vowed Tuesday to convey to the world that things are back to normal following parliament’s impeachment of conservative President Yoon Suk Yeol, as rival parties squabble the appointments of court justices who will determine whether to unseat or r
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ukrainian official takes credit after Russian general Igor Kirillov killed by explosive device in Moscow
item.description: An official with the Ukrainian Security Services says the agency was behind the bombing assassination of Lt. Gen. Igor Kirillov, commander of Russia’s chemical, biological and radiation defense forces, and his assistant in Moscow on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strike in Gaza kills at least 8 from the same family, Palestinians say
item.description: Palestinian medics say an Israeli strike in Gaza killed at least eight people from the same family, most of them women and children.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Australian state proposes ban on protests at places of worship to fight rising antisemitism
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — An Australian state government on Tuesday proposed new restrictions on protesters in response to rising antisemitism, including a ban on demonstrations outside places of worship.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Grassroots funding for Ukraine's military declines as groups look for creative ways to raise money
item.description: Advertisements pasted along the sidewalk in Kyiv ask for donations to help fill financial gaps across Ukraine's military.
item.dc:creator: Yehor Konovalov, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Death toll in Gaza from the Israel-Hamas war tops 45,000 Palestinians, health officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — More than 45,000 Palestinians have now been killed in the war in Gaza between Israel and Hamas militants, health officials in the embattled territory said Monday, as often-stalled ceasefire negotiations appeared to be gaining ground.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Magnitude 7.3 earthquake causes widespread damage in Pacific island nation of Vanuatu
item.description: A powerful magnitude 7.3 earthquake has struck just off the coast of Vanuatu, causing widespread destruction in the South Pacific island nation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: UN announces initiative to overcome political deadlock in Libya
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The top U.N. official in Libya announced a new initiative Monday to overcome a three-year deadlock and move the divided oil-rich North African nation toward a national election. The U.N. deputy special envoy for Libya, Stephanie Koury, told the U.N.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says Turkey ‘did an unfriendly takeover’ in Syria as US-brokered cease-fire appears to fail
item.description: President-elect Trump described the fall of the Assad regime in Syria as an "unfriendly takeover" by neighboring Turkey as sources tell Fox News Digital the U.S.-brokered cease-fire has collapsed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 26 people arrested in crackdown on illegal deforestation along Paraguay, Brazil and Argentina border
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Paraguayan authorities arrested 26 people in a crackdown on suspected illegal deforestation along the country's border with Brazil and Argentina, Interpol announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Palestinian security forces launch a rare crackdown on militants in the West Bank
item.description: JENIN, West Bank (AP) — Palestinian security forces have launched a rare crackdown against local militant groups in the northern West Bank, sending in armored cars and engaging in fierce gunbattles that have killed at least two people in the volatile area.
item.dc:creator: MAJDI MOHAMMED and AREF TUFAHA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says Russia-Ukraine peace may be harder than Middle East
item.description: President-elect Trump on Monday expressed confidence that he could settle the war between Russia and Ukraine but also stressed that it would be difficult and possibly harder to solve than the conflict between Israel and the Palestinian militant group Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Russian presence in Syria remains. But it's unclear for how long
item.description: HMEIMIM, Syria (AP) — A convoy of Russian military vehicles rolled down the highway towards the Syrian city of Tartus on Monday as soldiers stood guard. Planes periodically descended and rose from Russia’s Hmeimim air base in the Syrian coastal province of Latakia while smoke rose from the base.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine details North Korean casualties in Russia's Kursk region
item.description: Ukraine is reporting at least 30 North Korean soldiers were killed or wounded over the weekend around several villages on the front in Russia's Kursk region.
item.dc:creator: Joanne Haner, The Hill

item.title: Assad in first public comments since ouster says he wanted to keep fighting in Syria
item.description: Overthrown Syrian leader Bashar Assad said Monday in his first public comments since fleeing to Moscow that he wanted to keep fighting in Syria. In a statement posted to an official Telegram channel, Assad said Russia requested he leave Syria.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: FIFA apologizes to Ukraine soccer body for Crimea map error at 2026 World Cup draw
item.description: “While the incident was unintentional, we sincerely regret any concern it may have cause,” FIFA’s chief member association officer wrote in a letter .
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea’s impeached leader avoids investigators as court begins meeting to determine his fate
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol dodged requests by investigative agencies to appear for questioning over his short-lived martial law decree, as the Constitutional Court began its first meeting Monday on Yoon's case to determine whether to formally unseat or
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian paramilitary soldiers killed in friendly fire attack by North Koreans after enlisting DPRK help
item.description: North Korean troops recently killed Russian soldiers in a friendly fire attack, according to Ukrainian intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Filipina who won a last-minute reprieve from Indonesian firing squad prepares to fly home
item.description: A Filipina who was on death row in Indonesia and was nearly executed by firing squad in 2015 will return home this week under an arrangement between the countries.
item.dc:creator: Slamet Riyadi And Niniek Karmini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mother of an American journalist imprisoned in Syria sees hope following news of Travis Timmerman's release
item.description: The mother of Austin Tice, an American journalist captured in Syria, said the news of Travis Timmerman's recent prison release has sparked new hope that her son will soon walk free.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Christian leader in Lebanon urges US, allies to intervene to stop Hezbollah
item.description: The leader of a Christian coalition and political party in Lebanon is calling on the U.S. and its Western allies to deploy troops to Lebanon to help the army dismantle Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Capitol rioter who tried to join Russian army is sentenced to prison for probation violation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A Dallas man who tried to fly overseas to join the Russian military and fight against Ukraine was sentenced on Friday to six months in prison for violating the terms of his probation for storming the U.S. Capitol four years ago. Kevin Loftus, a 56-year-old veteran of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: New Zealand scientists suspect specimen of world’s rarest whale died from head injuries
item.description: After completing the dissection of the first complete specimen of the world's rarest whale, scientists suspect the 16-foot spade-toothed whale died from head injuries.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American who says he crossed into Syria by foot is free after 7 months
item.description: An American who turned up in Syria on Thursday says he was detained after crossing into the country by foot on a Christian pilgrimage seven months ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Coffee prices hit record high after challenging growing season
item.description: Arabica coffee sold for $3.44 a pound on the global market earlier this week, beating the prior record high of $3.35 set in 1977.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation
